name: Outeiro da Moura and A Coruxeira – circular from Vilares
name_original: Outeiro da Moura y A Coruxeira – circular desde Vilares
region_intro: Jogging around Allariz offers diverse routes through a landscape shaped by the Arnoia River and its extensive green areas. The region is part of the UNESCO Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, featuring varied habitats including plains, meadows, and oak stands. Joggers can explore well-maintained riverside paths, historic cobbled streets, and trails that traverse a mix of natural and cultural elements. This combination provides a range of running experiences from flat riverside stretches to more undulating terrain. Best jogging routes around Allariz The most popular jogging route is Outeiro da Moura and A Coruxeira – circular from Vilares, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local runners is Penamá Viewpoint – Fonte do Santo loop from Allariz, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. This route provides more challenging terrain with significant elevation gain, leading to scenic viewpoints. Local runners also love the Allariz Roman Bridge – Allariz loop from Allariz, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through the historic quarter and along the Arnoia River, often completed in about 49 minutes. Jogging around Allariz is defined by the Arnoia River, its extensive green areas, and the varied habitats of the Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more difficult trails with elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 runners have used komoot to explore Allariz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Allariz?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes around Allariz, catering to various fitness levels. These include 14 moderate trails and 11 more challenging routes, ensuring a diverse experience for every runner.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Allariz?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the extensive green areas along the Arnoia River offer flatter, well-maintained paths suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier run. The historic quarter also provides pleasant, less strenuous options.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Allariz?
A: Yes, many routes in Allariz are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990942865/outeiro-da-moura-and-a-coruxeira-circular-from-vilares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outeiro da Moura and A Coruxeira – circular from Vilares&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 4.7-mile (7.5 km) loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz Roman Bridge – Allariz loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; offers a 4.4-mile (7.1 km) run through the historic quarter and along the Arnoia River.

Q: What are the most scenic running trails in Allariz?
A: For scenic views, consider routes along the Arnoia River, which offer picturesque landscapes and historical elements like the medieval Vilanova Bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penamá Viewpoint – Fonte do Santo loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that rewards runners with significant elevation gain and stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any jogging routes that pass by waterfalls or other natural attractions?
A: Yes, you can incorporate natural attractions into your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area that can be reached via some trails. Additionally, the region is part of the UNESCO Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, offering diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are the running trails in Allariz suitable for families?
A: Many of the riverside paths along the Arnoia River are relatively flat and well-maintained, making them suitable for families. These areas often feature recreational grounds, providing a pleasant environment for a family jog or walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Allariz?
A: Allariz is generally dog-friendly, especially on its numerous outdoor trails. The extensive green areas and riverside paths are popular spots for dog walkers and runners. However, always ensure your dog is under control and check for any specific local regulations on certain routes or protected areas.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while running in Allariz?
A: As Allariz is part of the UNESCO Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, you might encounter diverse local flora and fauna. The reserve preserves habitats like 'veigas' (plains), 'touzas' (mosaics of meadows and croplands), and 'sebes' (mixed oak stands), which are home to various bird species and small mammals.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Absolutely. Allariz's well-preserved historic quarter offers routes past cobbled streets, stone houses, and historical monuments like the Church of Santiago and the remains of Allariz Castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Freixo Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another significant landmark you might encounter on your run.

Q: Is there parking available near the jogging routes in Allariz?
A: Allariz, being a town that welcomes outdoor activities, typically has parking options available, especially near the town center and popular starting points for riverside paths. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Allariz?
A: Allariz is pleasant for jogging throughout much of the year. The riverside paths along the Arnoia River are particularly cool and enjoyable during the summer months. Spring and autumn also offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced runners looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penamá Viewpoint – Fonte do Santo loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.4-mile (15.1 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Roimelo loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt;, covering 8.4 miles (13.5 km) with over 300 meters of elevation.
tips: Small parish located in the northwest of the municipality of Allariz. The temple, with a rectangular floor plan and a single nave, was built in 1640 using a granite slab.
Small parish in Allariz, in the province of Orense. The temple is also known as La Mezquita.
